title: Mobile Companion Workstream
subtitle: Repo-connect, repo-backed transport delivery, push runtime, EAS, disconnect, and persistence plan for the Living Docs mobile app.
scope: Mobile companion implementation work across tickets #19-#24 and #36, with #18 retained as closed historical context. The subscription/refinement product model is tracked separately in `docs/mobile-subscription-refinement-model.json`.
sourceCoverage: GitHub issues #18-#24 and #36 inspected through GitHub tooling on 2026-04-17, throwaway-repo and actual iPhone Expo Go preview verification rerun through GitHub tooling on 2026-04-19, plus repo-local mobile source tree, the grounded-event transport pass and first auto-generated grounded push verification landed on 2026-04-19, `docs/mobile-mockups.html`, and the coupled subscription/refinement model tracked in `docs/mobile-subscription-refinement-model.json`.
objective: Keep the mobile companion implementation coherent across UI fidelity, repo connection, repo-backed transport delivery, push-token runtime behavior, EAS setup, disconnect, persistence, and the handoff seam into the separate subscription/refinement model.
successCondition: A Codex worker can pick up the next ticket with dependencies, open decisions, affected files, and verification expectations visible in one document.

objectiveFacets.name: UI fidelity
objectiveFacets.description: Native surfaces match the mockup canvas at pixel level across screens.

objectiveFacets.name: Repo connection
objectiveFacets.description: Connect-a-repo flow: secret write, workflow install, status refresh.

objectiveFacets.name: Repo-backed transport delivery
objectiveFacets.description: Feed events written to the living-docs-feed branch and synced into the app.

objectiveFacets.name: Push-token runtime
objectiveFacets.description: Runtime classification and copy across web / Expo Go / EAS dev builds.

objectiveFacets.name: EAS setup
objectiveFacets.description: EAS build profiles, project ownership, and dev-build verification.

objectiveFacets.name: Disconnect
objectiveFacets.description: Clean removal of secret and optionally workflow, with idempotent status refresh.

objectiveFacets.name: Persistence
objectiveFacets.description: Device-flow session, repo-connection mode, and SQLite inbox event log across restarts.

objectiveFacets.name: Subscription seam
objectiveFacets.description: Handoff boundary into the separate subscription/refinement product model.

invariants.name: Mobile implementation cards declare where the work lives
invariants.statement: Every Mobile Capability Map and Runtime State Model card carries at least one codePaths entry so status connects to the source tree.

invariants.name: Tooling cards explain how to use themselves
invariants.statement: Every Operational Tools card declares purpose, whenToUse, and invocation — the instrument layer is self-describing.

invariants.name: Verification gates carry current coverage, next step, and gaps
invariants.statement: Every Verification Plan card declares currentCoverage, nextStep, and gaps so the next tester sees what is proven and what is still open.

invariants.name: Subscription model is tracked in a sibling doc
invariants.statement: Subscription/refinement product model belongs in docs/mobile-subscription-refinement-model.json; this doc tracks implementation and runtime truth only.

invariants.name: Decisions stay separate from implementation and verification
invariants.statement: Ground-truth operating choices live in Open Decisions, not inside capability or verification cards.

callouts.title: How to use this doc
items: Use #19-#24 and #36 as the current transport/runtime implementation source of truth. Treat #18 as closed context only.
Use `docs/mobile-subscription-refinement-model.json` for the target subscription/refinement model across #27/#28/#31/#32/#33/#35.
Before editing `mobile/src/navigation.tsx` or `mobile/src/screens/Repos.tsx`, inspect the existing local worktree changes so unrelated edits are not overwritten.

sections.title: Objective Coherence
sections.rationale: Projects the doc-root objective facets, coverage, and invariants so coverage and governance stay visible next to the implementation work.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Current state
items: The repo-backed delivery spine is now proven end to end: a connected repo can install the workflow, write feed events to `living-docs-feed`, and the phone can sync those events into the app without depending on Apple push.
This document is now the implementation and runtime snapshot only. The detailed subscription/refinement product model lives in `docs/mobile-subscription-refinement-model.json`.
The current Inbox and Home surfaces should be treated as transport and audit infrastructure until the separate subscription model is implemented.

Delivery now depends on a small repo-local manifest at `.living-docs/manifest.json`, while the central library remains the richer cross-system catalog. The two need shared document identity rather than pretending one can replace the other.

The transport boundary can now carry richer grounded/block fields without breaking old feed items, and a real repo-side generator now emits structured section/card events from manifest-backed doc diffs. The next implementation slice is sync hardening plus richer commit/session artifacts so grounding can move beyond warning-level diff inference.
The remaining hard gates are pixel-match verification, real paid-team push verification, native Inbox persistence verification, and sync hardening.

sections.rationale: Compresses seven open tickets, four blocking gates, and the current pressure pattern into one glance so the doc opens on state, not on narrative.

sections.title: Mobile Capability Map
sections.rationale: Seven parallel subsystems — auth, home, settings, repo connect, disconnect, delivery feed, inbox — need side-by-side comparability; a single narrative lane would hide drift between them.

data.name: Authentication and sign-in surface
notes: The app has authentication code and a sign-in screen, with the native idle, code-issued, and polling states now aligned closer to the mockup spacing, button placement, code-card padding, and polling typography.
The implementation now treats native device-flow login as the only supported GitHub sign-in path. Browser preview shows an explicit limitation state until a proper web OAuth callback flow exists.
Pending native device-flow sessions are now persisted locally and resume after the GitHub browser hop, so Expo Go no longer forces the user to restart sign-in after a successful approval.

data.name: Home screen and document registry
notes: The screen exists and can list available living documents.
The #19 code-alignment pass added the 72x72 empty-state icon tile, exact empty copy, and 14x20 document card spacing.
#18 is useful context for why the mobile app exists, but its scaffolding should not be treated as the active implementation ticket.

data.name: Settings runtime and push status
notes: `mobile/src/notifications.ts` now exposes `pushRuntime()` with the ticket-defined `eas`, `expo-go`, `web`, and `unknown` type contract.
Settings now shows runtime-specific push status copy for native builds, Expo Go, and web preview mode instead of silently degrading when no push token exists.
The failure copy for native builds no longer assumes the only problem is permission denial; it now points at permission, project setup, and rebuild issues as separate real causes.
The #19 code-alignment pass tightened section-label spacing, row padding, info-label sizing, and subtitle line-height toward the mockup.

data.name: Connect-a-repo flow
notes: A real throwaway-repo pass is now proven against `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346`: the app-shaped connect contract created `EXPO_PUSH_TOKEN`, wrote `.github/workflows/living-doc-notify.yml` via commit `2ba6418`, restored a drifted workflow via commit `aec8ce9`, and successfully executed manual run `24629243483` plus push-triggered run `24629263428`.

A real iPhone Expo Go pass on 2026-04-19 now proves the preview-mode UI: the row rendered `Wired for preview` / `preview-connected` for `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346`, and the connected repo immediately recorded push run `24629696669` from the workflow-install commit.
Browser preview now presents repo connection as unavailable until a proper web OAuth callback flow exists. Native builds remain the only supported path for GitHub auth and repo wiring.
Connection status now treats the workflow file in the Contents API as the source of truth instead of depending on GitHub Actions workflow-list indexing.
After connect and disconnect mutations, Repos now retries status briefly before rendering the final row state and only shows success copy when the observed status matches the requested mutation.
When no real push token is available, Repos now writes the explicit placeholder token and shows warning-toned preview copy instead of claiming full push delivery.

Connection mode is now persisted locally per repo. Explicitly preview-connected rows show an Upgrade action when a real push token becomes available, while unknown connected rows can still Reconnect from a real-token runtime to overwrite opaque GitHub secrets.
The #19 code-alignment pass removed the extra in-screen title so the stack header owns the page title and tightened row typography/button dimensions.

data.name: Disconnect repo flow
notes: `mobile/src/github-api.ts` now exposes `deleteRepoSecret`, `deleteWorkflowFile`, and `disconnectRepo` with missing-secret and missing-workflow cases treated idempotently.
Connected and preview-connected rows now expose an ellipsis action that can remove only the secret or remove both the secret and workflow file.
The row refreshes connection status after a successful disconnect, so secret-only removal should show `Partial` when the workflow remains.

A 2026-04-19 throwaway-repo pass proved both disconnect modes on `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346`: secret-only removal left workflow/manual feed writes working in run `24629278524`, while workflow removal commit `2f15e98` stopped later tracked-path push `818e06e` from creating any new run or feed file.

data.name: Repo-backed in-app delivery feed
notes: The workflow contract now writes one JSON event per run into the `living-docs-feed` branch under `.living-docs/feed/`, and Expo push is treated as optional best-effort acceleration.

A repo-local delivery manifest now exists at `.living-docs/manifest.json`; it mirrors delivery-critical fields from the central registry so push-time automation can map changed doc paths to doc ids, titles, and public URLs.
The mobile app now syncs feed events from connected repos on app launch, foreground resume, and pull-to-refresh in Home or Inbox.
Feed events are ingested through the same Inbox/doc-registration path as notifications, so docs can appear in the app even when Apple or Expo push is unavailable.

The feed contract is now backward-compatible but richer: `mobile/src/delivery-feed.ts` parses optional `schemaVersion`, `kind`, `transition`, `intent`, `evidence`, `grounding`, `openQuestions`, and `blocks[]` fields while still accepting the original doc-level payload shape.

The workflow template now accepts structured `eventJson` on `workflow_dispatch` and direct `event` / `events` objects on `repository_dispatch`, so repo-side senders can hand the app grounded/block artifacts immediately instead of waiting for a future transport rewrite.

Repo-side verification now exists against `triadflow/mobile-connect-test`: workflow run `24510668921` created branch `living-docs-feed` and file `2026-04-16T12-39-10Z--triadflow-mobile-connect-test-24510668921-1.json` with the expected payload, and a second throwaway-repo proof now exists against `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` via manual run `24629243483`, push run `24629263428`, and secret-removed run `24629278524`.

The current workflow now also triggers on `push` and inspects changed manifest-backed doc paths on the default branch. It embeds a small Node helper that prefers explicit commit artifacts from `.living-doc/commits/<sha>.json` or `.living-docs/commits/<sha>.json` and otherwise heuristically derives section/card block events from before/after living-doc JSON.

Actual phone-side preview delivery is now proven on `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346`: manual runs `24629714376` and `24629722084` produced `Phone preview verification event` and `Phone preview verification event 13:01`, and the connected iPhone Inbox showed both after refresh.

A 2026-04-19 manual structured grounded-event proof on `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` used workflow run `24630301322` to write `Grounded smoke-doc verification 13:32`, and the connected iPhone Inbox rendered the richer block row correctly.

Automatic push-triggered delivery is now also proven end to end on that same throwaway repo: tracked-doc commit `b1013ee` triggered run `24630808033`, wrote feed file `2026-04-19T13-59-18Z--triadflow-mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346-24630808033-1-1.json`, and the iPhone Inbox showed `Repo preview loop updated` with `ready -> grounded` after refresh.

Phone-side verification is therefore proven for manual feed sync, manual structured grounded events, and automatic grounded push generation. `triadflow/living-doc-compositor` also still proves plain automatic push-triggered feed creation via workflow run `24512708555` from commit `6861d5a`.

This transport feed is now good enough to serve as the spine for the next UX layer; the remaining gap is richer repo-native intent/session artifacts and sync hardening, not basic grounded-row delivery.

data.name: Persistent inbox and notification audit trail
notes: Notifications now persist through `expo-sqlite` in `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts` instead of a module-scoped array.
Notification capture has moved to `mobile/App.tsx`, while the Inbox screen subscribes to the store and renders the event log.
Rows support read state, swipe-to-delete, and a clear-all action with confirmation.
The #19 code-alignment pass added the empty notification icon tile, 14x20 row spacing, and relative-time/source metadata.
#36 has now extended this store into the destination for synced GitHub feed events, so app-visible updates no longer depend on OS notification delivery.

A 2026-04-19 real iPhone Expo Go pass confirmed that repo-synced events from `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` appeared in Inbox after pull-to-refresh, including `Phone preview verification event` and `Phone preview verification event 13:01`.

The same device pass later proved richer rows too: `Grounded smoke-doc verification 13:32` rendered from manual structured run `24630301322`, and `Repo preview loop updated` rendered from auto-generated push run `24630808033`.

Inbox rows now render richer feed context when present: block/audience strip, transition and grounding pills, and summary lines for block summary, intent, evidence count, and open-question count while staying compatible with older feed items.

The persisted Inbox should now be understood as transport and audit infrastructure. Under the newer subscription UX, the eventual primary feed will sit on top of richer block-level events and refinement state rather than exposing raw doc-update messages directly.

sections.title: Runtime and Connection State Model
sections.rationale: Web / Expo Go / EAS / repo-artifact / local-stores read as one operating lane of the mobile runtime; each state depends on the previous and a parallel grid would shatter the sequence.

data.name: Web runtime
notes: `pushRuntime()` now returns `web` when `Platform.OS === 'web'`.
Settings and Repos now treat web as browser-preview-only and block repo setup until a proper web OAuth callback flow exists.

data.name: Expo Go runtime
notes: `pushRuntime()` now classifies Expo Go conservatively from `Constants.executionEnvironment === storeClient` plus Expo Go-specific constants instead of relying on the deprecated `appOwnership` flag alone.
Settings and Repos label Expo Go as preview-only and point the user toward an EAS dev build for real pushes.
Settings now includes a local preview-notification action so Expo Go can still exercise inbox capture and doc deep-link handling without a real Expo push token.

A 2026-04-19 real iPhone Expo Go session confirmed the preview-only repo path end to end: `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` connected as `preview-connected`, the app showed `Wired for preview`, and manual repo events landed in Inbox after refresh.
The workspace dependency baseline is now Expo SDK 54 / React Native 0.81 / React 19.1, and `npx expo start --go --tunnel` launches successfully.

data.name: EAS/dev-build runtime
notes: `mobile/eas.json` now defines development, preview, and production profiles.

`mobile/README.md` now documents the committed shared Triadflow Expo project, fork-local `eas init`, iOS and Android development builds, install flow, first-launch permission, and Settings verification.

`mobile/package.json` now includes `expo-dev-client` plus explicit `npm run web`, `npm run go`, `npm run dev-client`, and `npm run ios:preview` scripts so web preview, Expo Go, development-build testing, and iOS preview-only local builds are distinct entry points.
`mobile/plugins/withPreviewNotificationsOnly.js` strips remote-notification entitlements from `npm run ios:preview` builds so local iOS UI work stays separate from push-capable development builds.

`mobile/app.json` now commits the shared Triadflow Expo owner/projectId for the canonical repo; forks should replace that locally instead of writing a personal project binding back into source control.

data.name: GitHub repo artifact status
notes: Repo status distinguishes missing workflow, missing secret, partial setup, and ready setup where possible.
GitHub secrets are write-only, so secret presence and secret value cannot be treated as a reliable readable source of truth after creation.
Disconnect now reuses the same status refresh path after removing the secret and optionally deleting `.github/workflows/living-doc-notify.yml`.
The workflow contract now also writes feed-event JSON files into the `living-docs-feed` branch, which gives the app a GitHub-backed source of truth to sync from.
Repo-side verification has been proven on `triadflow/mobile-connect-test`, where the updated workflow created the feed branch and wrote a valid JSON event file.

data.name: Local mobile state stores
notes: #21 and #20 now use `mobile/src/repo-connection-store.ts` to preserve preview-vs-real repo connection mode across refreshes on the same app install.
#24 now introduces `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts` as an application-level SQLite event log rather than screen-local state.
#36 now reuses these stores directly: connected repos provide the sync target list, and the Inbox store is the dedupe and persistence sink for synced repo feed entries.

sections.title: Operational Tools and Artifacts
sections.rationale: GitHub API client, sealed-box crypto, workflow template, mockup canvas, EAS build, inbox store — the instrument layer used to operate on the mobile domain, each with its own purpose, when-to-use, and invocation.

data.name: GitHub API client
data.purpose: Create, inspect, remove, and eventually sync GitHub repository artifacts for the mobile companion connection and delivery feed.
data.whenToUse: Use when implementing or verifying connect, status refresh, disconnect behavior, or repo-backed feed sync.
data.invocation: Called by the mobile app screens, not directly from the CLI.
notes: The API surface should be verified against a real throwaway repository before being treated as trusted.
The client now includes the disconnect side of the lifecycle: delete secret, delete workflow file, and refresh status.
Workflow status now comes from the Contents API file at `.github/workflows/living-doc-notify.yml`, which avoids eventual-consistency lag in the Actions workflow-list endpoint.
The client now extends beyond connect/disconnect/status work into reading unseen feed artifacts from connected repositories.
caveats: GitHub secret values are not readable after write, so status logic needs a separate source for preview or real-token state.

data.name: GitHub secret sealed-box encryption
data.purpose: Encrypt the push token for GitHub Actions secrets using GitHub's public key flow.
data.whenToUse: Use during connect-repo verification and any debugging of 422 or malformed-secret errors.
data.invocation: Called from `mobile/src/github-api.ts` when writing the repository secret.
caveats: The implementation now asserts GitHub's public key is 32 bytes and the sealed ciphertext length is `message + 48` bytes before sending it to the REST API.
If GitHub rejects secret creation, first validate sealed-box output, UTF-8/base64 handling, key IDs, and public-key fetch behavior.

data.name: Living-doc notification workflow template
data.purpose: Install the GitHub Actions workflow that writes repo-backed delivery events and optionally attempts Expo Push acceleration when a living document changes.
data.whenToUse: Use in connect, status refresh, disconnect, and delivery-feed work where workflow contents or path behavior matter.
data.invocation: Written through GitHub Contents API by the mobile app.
caveats: Disconnect semantics need to define whether the workflow is removed, disabled, or left in place without a secret.

The workflow can now accept richer structured `eventJson` / `event` / `events` payloads and preserve them into the feed. Its built-in `push` path now packages an embedded Node helper into GitHub Actions, prefers explicit commit artifacts when present, and otherwise heuristically derives section/card block events from manifest-backed living-doc JSON diffs.

The default generator still does not read session-memory intent artifacts or refinement state, so automatically derived grounding remains warning-level even when the resulting Inbox row is structurally rich.

data.name: Mobile mockup canvas
data.purpose: Provide the visual reference for pixel-matching the mobile screens.
data.whenToUse: Use before editing any mobile screen layout or theme work covered by #19.
data.invocation: Open `docs/mobile-mockups.html` and compare against the running app.
caveats: The current tickets do not define an automated screenshot diff harness, so verification is still manual unless one is added.

data.name: EAS dev-build setup
data.purpose: Make real native push-token testing reproducible outside Expo Go.
data.whenToUse: Use after #21 labels runtime modes and before claiming push notifications are production-real.
data.invocation: `cd mobile && npx eas-cli build --profile development --platform ios` or `npx eas-cli build --profile development --platform android`.
caveats: Requires an Expo account and `npx eas-cli init` before a contributor can build.
No successful paid-team push-capable device build has been recorded in `mobile/README.md` yet.

data.name: SQLite inbox store
data.purpose: Persist notification events and user-read state for the mobile Inbox.
data.whenToUse: Use when implementing the persistent Inbox and when deciding where notification listeners write events.
data.invocation: Imported by application-level notification handling and `mobile/src/screens/Inbox.tsx`.
caveats: Typecheck passes, but restart persistence, swipe gestures, and notification response navigation still need manual device/runtime verification.

sections.title: Verification Plan
sections.rationale: Seven in-progress gates each need rich evidence — current coverage, next step, and gaps — because the work is still landing and the next tester must see exactly what is left.

data.name: Pixel-match mobile screens
data.currentCoverage: A bounded #19 code pass aligned Home and Inbox empty icon tiles, Home/Inbox row and header spacing, Repos intro and row/button typography, Settings label/row details, Inbox relative-time/source metadata, native Sign In state spacing, and centered Doc Detail title behavior. `npm run typecheck` passes.
data.nextStep: Run the mobile app at an equivalent 393x852 viewport and compare Sign In, Home, Doc Detail, Settings, Repos, and Inbox against `docs/mobile-mockups.html`.
notes: This should remain a verification gate because visual parity cannot be proven from code inspection alone.
gaps: No explicit device/simulator capture workflow is documented in the ticket.
No pixel-diff threshold or artifact storage path is defined.

data.name: Connect-a-repo end-to-end
data.currentCoverage: A 2026-04-19 throwaway-repo pass against `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` verified preview-token secret creation, workflow create commit `2ba6418`, drift commit `c2ca2a3`, restore commit `aec8ce9`, manual workflow run `24629243483`, and push-triggered run `24629263428` with feed artifacts written to `living-docs-feed`. A same-day real iPhone Expo Go pass then connected the same repo in preview mode, rendered `Wired for preview` / `preview-connected` in-app, and the repo recorded workflow-install push run `24629696669`. The repo-side connect contract and preview-mode phone UI are now proven.
data.nextStep: Capture the same connect flow from a real EAS/dev build if the team wants the native push-ready row state and toasts on record; repo-side correctness is no longer the blocker.
gaps: These passes used Expo Go preview mode or placeholder tokens, so they do not prove real native Expo push delivery.
No native push-ready Repos-screen capture from an EAS/dev build has been recorded yet.

data.name: Repo-backed in-app delivery sync
data.currentCoverage: The workflow template now writes one JSON event per run into `.living-docs/feed/` on the `living-docs-feed` branch, and the app syncs those files from connected repos on launch, foreground resume, and pull-to-refresh into the shared Inbox/doc-ingestion path. `npm run typecheck` passes. A repo-local delivery manifest contract now exists at `.living-docs/manifest.json`, and the workflow reacts to `push` on the default branch by resolving changed manifest-backed doc paths into feed events. The feed contract can now also carry optional `schemaVersion`, `kind`, `transition`, `intent`, `evidence`, `grounding`, `openQuestions`, and `blocks[]` fields. The workflow accepts structured `eventJson` or `repository_dispatch` `event` / `events` payloads, and its built-in `push` path now embeds a repo-side generator that prefers explicit commit artifacts and otherwise derives section/card block events from before/after living-doc JSON. Repo-side verification has been proven against `triadflow/mobile-connect-test`, where workflow run `24510668921` created the feed branch and file `2026-04-16T12-39-10Z--triadflow-mobile-connect-test-24510668921-1.json` with the expected payload. A second throwaway-repo proof now exists on `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346`, where manual run `24629243483`, push run `24629263428`, and secret-removed run `24629278524` each wrote the expected feed artifact. Real iPhone Expo Go verification on that same repo now covers three lanes: manual feed sync (`24629714376`, `24629722084`), manual structured grounded events (`24630301322`), and automatic grounded push generation from tracked-doc commit `b1013ee` via run `24630808033`, which wrote `Repo preview loop updated` with `ready -> grounded` and rendered correctly in the iPhone Inbox after refresh. Phone-side verification is also proven against `triadflow/trackandback-lead`, where the connected repo produced a feed event and the app showed it in Inbox after refresh. Automatic push-triggered delivery is also proven in `triadflow/living-doc-compositor`, where push commit `6861d5a` triggered workflow run `24512708555` and wrote a new feed event file without any manual dispatch.
data.nextStep: Keep the heuristic generator stable, add a small explicit commit/session artifact format for repos that can emit richer intent, and harden sync with per-repo cursors or watermarks.
gaps: Repeated syncs currently rely on Inbox primary-key dedupe rather than a per-repo cursor or watermark.

The default generator currently grounds from manifest-backed JSON diff and optional commit artifacts only; it does not yet read session-memory intent or user refinement state, so grounding stays warning-level.
There is not yet a helper to derive or refresh the repo-local manifest automatically from the richer central registry.
The refinement overlay and user-scoped write path from #27 do not exist yet.

data.name: Push-token runtime matrix
data.currentCoverage: `pushRuntime()` exists, Settings consumes it, and Repos now differentiates real and preview connection paths with distinct copy, toast tone, and row pills. `npm run typecheck` passes. A 2026-04-19 real iPhone Expo Go pass now verifies the preview lane in practice: the app connected `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` as `preview-connected`, showed `Wired for preview`, and still synced repo-feed events into Inbox.
data.nextStep: Verify the remaining web and EAS/dev-build lanes, then update #21 with runtime screenshots or notes.
gaps: GitHub secrets cannot prove after the fact whether a stored value was a real Expo token or a placeholder.
The real push-ready EAS/dev-build lane is still unverified on device.
This implementation keeps preview state explicit in the current runtime and current session; a durable readable preview marker is still deferred unless #20 or #23 requires it.

data.name: EAS dev-build verification
data.currentCoverage: `mobile/eas.json` exists, `mobile/README.md` documents the real-push build flow, and `npm run typecheck` passes.
data.nextStep: Run `npx eas-cli init`, produce an iOS or Android development build, verify Settings shows `Ready...`, and add a `Verified EAS build on YYYY-MM-DD` note to `mobile/README.md`.
gaps: No real EAS development build has been recorded yet.
A real device build requires Expo credentials outside the repo.

data.name: Disconnect repo verification
data.currentCoverage: A 2026-04-19 throwaway-repo pass against `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` verified both disconnect modes: deleting only `EXPO_PUSH_TOKEN` left `.github/workflows/living-doc-notify.yml` in place and run `24629278524` still wrote a feed event without push delivery, while deleting the workflow via commit `2f15e98` left the repo with no workflow and later tracked-doc push `818e06e` produced no new run or feed file.
data.nextStep: If needed, capture the same flow from the native Repos action sheet; repo-side disconnect semantics are now proven.
gaps: No native Repos-screen capture of the destructive action sheet has been recorded yet.

data.name: Persistent inbox verification
data.currentCoverage: `expo-sqlite` is installed, `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts` exists, App-level notification ingestion writes the store, Inbox supports list, mark-read, swipe delete, and clear-all, and both preview notifications and repo-feed sync now land through the same ingest path. `npm run typecheck` passes. A 2026-04-19 real iPhone Expo Go pass now proves repo-feed ingestion and richer row rendering into Inbox on device: `Phone preview verification event` and `Phone preview verification event 13:01` from `triadflow/mobile-connect-e2e-20260419-123346` both appeared after refresh, `Grounded smoke-doc verification 13:32` rendered the manual structured row, and `Repo preview loop updated` from auto-generated push run `24630808033` rendered the same richer UI from a normal tracked-doc change.
data.nextStep: Verify restart persistence, tap-to-read navigation, swipe delete, and clear-all on device now that repo-feed ingestion is proven.
gaps: Restart persistence and the destructive Inbox actions still lack recorded device verification.
The ticket still does not define retention or pruning for old events.

sections.title: Open Decisions
sections.rationale: Authoritative operating choices kept compact and settled, with page-status rather than block-status so they are not re-argued on every ticket.

data.name: #18 is closed context, not active implementation scope
notes: #18 remains useful for intent and history, but #19-#24 are the actual work breakdown.

data.name: GitHub feed is the delivery source of truth; push is optional acceleration
notes: The product goal is that living-doc updates appear inside the app itself whenever the app refreshes, even if Apple or Expo push is unavailable.
Connected-repo workflow runs should therefore write a structured GitHub-backed feed that the app syncs into Inbox and the local doc registry.
Expo Push remains useful for waking the user or device, but correctness for in-app visibility cannot depend on paid-team push readiness.
The central living-doc registry should remain the richer cross-system catalog, but commit-time automation cannot depend on a local `~/.gtd` file or other central-only state.
Each connected repo therefore needs a minimal repo-local manifest that mirrors delivery-critical fields from the central registry, such as doc id, tracked JSON/HTML paths, title, and public URL.

The central registry remains authoritative for semantic identity and broader system discovery, while the repo-local manifest becomes authoritative for resolving changed files into delivery events during GitHub `push` workflows.
That repo-local manifest contract now exists in this repo at `.living-docs/manifest.json`, and the workflow template now consumes it on `push`.

The current Inbox proves this transport layer, but the newer subscription UX makes clear that the final product surface is a refinement feed of grounded block events, not a raw list of doc-update messages.

data.name: Keep implementation/runtime truth separate from the subscription model
notes: This workstream now tracks current code, runtime behavior, repo artifacts, and verification gates only.

The target subscription/refinement product model now lives in `docs/mobile-subscription-refinement-model.json` so the implementation doc does not collapse transport truth and target UX into one mixed surface.
Keep only enough reference here to explain the transport boundary and why Inbox remains an audit sink until the model doc lands in code.

data.name: Choose how preview connection state survives refresh
notes: Because GitHub secrets are write-only, the app cannot infer real-vs-placeholder token state from the secret value after creation.
The chosen implementation is local metadata in `mobile/src/repo-connection-store.ts`, keyed by repository full name and storing `real` or `preview` plus an ISO timestamp.
This avoids writing an additional readable repo marker while still letting the app upgrade a known preview connection when a real push token becomes available.
If the app is reinstalled or local storage is cleared, GitHub's opaque secret remains unknowable; from a real-token runtime, the row can still Reconnect to overwrite the secret.

data.name: Own notification capture above the Inbox screen
notes: Notification capture now lives in `mobile/App.tsx` so events can be persisted even when the Inbox screen is not mounted.
The Inbox screen renders and mutates persisted events through `mobile/src/inbox-store.ts`; it no longer owns the push-capture side effect.

data.name: Decide EAS project ownership before committing canonical config
notes: The canonical repo now commits the shared Triadflow Expo binding in `mobile/app.json` (`owner: triadflow` plus `extra.eas.projectId`).
That shared binding is the right default for the main repo; fork or contributor testing can replace it locally, but should not commit a personal Expo project ID back into shared source control.

data.name: A single GitHub secret models one active mobile endpoint
notes: The current secret-based connection model appears to support one notification target per connected repository unless the workflow and token model are expanded.
After #36, this remains a push-delivery limitation, but it no longer blocks updates from appearing inside the app because the repo feed becomes the source of truth.
Multi-device or team notification delivery should remain out of scope unless a new ticket defines it.
